mnemonics: Punctuation Police: Java uses semicolons (;) to end statements, commas (,) to separate items, and periods (.) to access members - just like punctuation in sentences!
keyPoints: Commas (,) separate parameters and array elements
Periods (.) access object members and package hierarchy
Colons (:) used in enhanced for loops and ternary operators
commonMistakes: Using commas instead of semicolons in for loops
Confusing dot operator with other separators
